text: HTTPS encrypts data between a user's browser and a website, preventing hackers from stealing sensitive information. For authentication, strong passwords and two-factor login help verify user identities. Input validation is equally important—websites should check all user-provided data for unexpected characters or scripts that could cause errors. Common vulnerabilities include SQL injection, where attackers manipulate database queries, and cross-site scripting (XSS), which injects malicious code into webpages. By applying these protective measures against such threats, developers can create safer, more reliable web applications for everyone.
